nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Enforce full argument allowlist for terminal commands**

The terminal validator only checks the command plus the first argument (`firstArg`) against the shared policy, so it allows materially different invocations than the GUI specs. For example, because `git prune --dry-run` is in the shared specs, `git prune --expire now` is accepted and executed; `git prune -h` documents `-n/--dry-run` as “do not remove, show only,” so dropping that flag makes the command destructive. This breaks the intended “same whitelist” guarantee and permits unsafe operations through the terminal path.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Avoid emitting duplicate sync-blocked security events**

In the out-of-sync path, `runTerminalPanelCommand` appends a `terminal_sync_blocked` event before calling `finalizeBlocked`, but `finalizeBlocked` always appends another blocked event. That causes one blocked command to produce two sync-blocked incidents (seq 1 and seq 2), which can double-count security events and distort downstream audit/alert metrics.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

## [Bundle](https://app.codecov.io/gh/Doogie201/promptops-gui/pull/22?dropdown=bundle) Report

Bundle size has no change :white_check_mark:

Addressed codex review threads in commit 7a19df9: (1) full-argument shared allowlist enforcement via exact spec-signature matching, and (2) single sync-block security event emission path. Evidence: docs/sprints/S15/evidence/at/AT-S15-02_blocked_security_event.json, docs/sprints/S15/evidence/at/AT-S15-03_sync_gate.json, docs/sprints/S15/evidence/gates/20_precommit_all.txt.
